judgment.summary: Okta offers strong cash generation and high gross margins but is tempered by high leverage and decelerating revenue growth.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last reported EPS of 0.90 beat the 0.85 estimate (6.3% surprise); next earnings date is 2026-05-28.
positives: PEG ratio 0.85 — suggests the stock is undervalued relative to its growth.
Forward P/E 19.14 vs trailing 61.87 — indicates significant expected earnings expansion.
Gross margin 77.4% — shows high profitability on core services.
Free cash flow (TTM) $836.12M — reflects strong ability to generate cash.
Total cash $2.55B vs total debt $451M — represents a very healthy liquidity position.
negatives: Debt/Equity 6.44 — reflects a high level of financial leverage.
Revenue growth 11.6% — shows relatively modest top-line expansion.
Analyst growth est for current Q -0.8% — points to a potential near-term growth slowdown.
Return on assets 1.0% — indicates low efficiency in utilizing assets to generate profit.

news.paragraph: Okta shares have recently faced downward pressure, sliding even as the broader S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 reach record highs. While Argus Research recently raised its price target to $75.00, recent reports highlight that the stock has been falling. Traders should consider this recent price weakness and the stock's performance relative to the broader market.
